New Braunfels, Texas — Buyer Resources

Buyer Rebate Program in New Braunfels — Keep More of What You Earn

1.5% of the purchase price returned to you at closing. Every home. Including new construction.

Call (830) 632-5725 Browse Listings

What is a buyer rebate — and why does it matter?

When you buy a home, the seller pays both their agent's commission and your agent's commission. In most transactions, your agent keeps the full amount. At Weichert Realtors — Corwin & Associates, we believe a portion of that belongs back in your pocket.

Our buyer rebate program returns 1.5% of the purchase price to you at closing. On a $350,000 home, that's $5,250. On a $500,000 home, it's $7,500. It shows up as a credit on your closing disclosure — reducing your out-of-pocket costs at the closing table or putting money back in your account.

We're a veteran-owned Weichert brokerage in New Braunfels. Our founder, Joe Corwin, built this practice around the idea that clients should walk away from every transaction knowing they got a fair deal. The rebate is part of that commitment — not a gimmick to win business, but a policy we've run since day one.

How much will you receive?

The rebate is straightforward: 1.5% of the final purchase price, credited at closing.

Purchase PriceYour Rebate (1.5%)
$300,000$4,500
$350,000$5,250
$400,000$6,000
$450,000$6,750
$500,000$7,500
$600,000$9,000

The rebate is applied at closing — you don't receive a check separate from the transaction. It reduces your net closing costs or the amount you bring to the table. Your lender and title company are both aware of it and will document it on the closing disclosure.

Yes, it applies to new construction

Builder sales offices pay a buyer's agent commission the same way private sellers do. When you purchase a new-build home through Weichert Corwin, our buyer rebate applies in full — 1.5% of your purchase price, credited at closing.

This is important in New Braunfels, where new construction communities are active across Canyon Lake, Spring Branch, and the broader Comal County area. Many buyers walk into a builder's sales office unrepresented and assume it saves them money. It doesn't — the commission is already built into the price. Having us represent you costs you nothing and gives you the rebate back at closing.

Stacking the rebate with a VA loan

If you're a veteran or active-duty service member, our VA home loan guide explains how the two programs work together. In short: they stack.

Veteran buying a $400,000 home — combined savings

$0 down
VA loan — no down payment required vs. $12,000–$80,000 conventional
No PMI
~$175/month saved vs. conventional with less than 20% down
$6,000
Buyer rebate at closing — 1.5% of $400,000

Between the $0 down payment, no PMI, and the rebate credit at closing, most veteran buyers come to the table with minimal or zero out-of-pocket costs. That's a real difference — and it's why veteran-owned representation matters more than most buyers realize.

The rebate process, step by step

  1. Call or contact us

    Before you start searching in earnest, let's talk. We'll confirm the rebate amount for your target price range, answer any questions, and pair you with the right agent for your needs.

  2. Sign a buyer representation agreement

    Texas law requires a written agreement before an agent can show homes. The rebate is documented in this agreement — no fine print, no conditions beyond the standard closing.

  3. Search, tour, and make an offer

    We handle everything a traditional buyer's agent handles: access to all MLS listings, tours, offer strategy, negotiation, inspections, and transaction management. Our job is to get you to the closing table on the best terms available. Explore our Canyon Lake, Gruene, Spring Branch, and Seguin neighborhood guides to start narrowing your search.

  4. Close — and see the credit on your disclosure

    At closing, your 1.5% rebate appears as a credit on the closing disclosure. Your net costs go down. You get your keys. That's it.

First-time buyers and the rebate

For first-time buyers, the rebate can be a meaningful offset against closing costs. In New Braunfels, closing costs typically run 2–3% of the purchase price — which on a $350,000 home means $7,000 to $10,500 due at the table. A $5,250 rebate covers the majority of that. Paired with programs like TSAHC or USDA loans (available in parts of Comal and Guadalupe counties), the rebate makes ownership significantly more accessible.

See our complete first-time buyer guide for a breakdown of loan programs, down payment assistance, and what to expect in the New Braunfels market in 2026.

Why this brokerage

Weichert Realtors — Corwin & Associates is a veteran-owned brokerage at 133 N Seguin Ave, New Braunfels. Joe Corwin served over 20 years in the U.S. Air Force before building this team. The same values that ran his service — discipline, honesty, finishing the job — run the brokerage.

The rebate isn't a discount-service model. Our agents are full-service, experienced, and active in the New Braunfels market every week. You get everything a traditional buyer's agent provides, plus the rebate at closing.

We serve New Braunfels, Canyon Lake, Spring Branch, Seguin, and the surrounding Hill Country area.

Claim your buyer rebate

1.5% back at closing. Every home. Including new construction. Call us to get started.

Frequently asked questions

A buyer rebate is a portion of the buyer's agent commission returned to you — the buyer — at closing. Instead of your agent keeping the full commission paid by the seller, part of it comes back to you as a credit on your closing disclosure. At Weichert Corwin, we return 1.5% of the purchase price to every buyer we represent.
We rebate 1.5% of the purchase price back to you at closing. On a $350,000 home, that's $5,250 returned to you. On a $500,000 home, it's $7,500. The rebate appears as a credit on your closing disclosure and reduces your out-of-pocket costs at closing.
Yes. The buyer rebate applies to resale homes and new construction purchases alike. Builder sales offices pay a buyer's agent commission the same way private sellers do. You receive the same 1.5% rebate at closing regardless of whether the home is existing or newly built.
Yes. VA loan buyers are eligible for our buyer rebate program. Your $0 down payment and no-PMI savings stack with the rebate at closing. For a veteran buying a $400,000 home, you come to the table with no down payment, pay no monthly PMI, and receive a $6,000 rebate credit. It's the most cost-effective way to buy a home in Texas.
Yes. Texas is one of the states that explicitly permits real estate rebates to buyers. The Texas Real Estate Commission (TREC) allows licensed brokers to rebate commissions to their clients. Our program is fully compliant with TREC rules and is disclosed in writing before you make an offer.

Ready to claim your rebate?

Veteran-owned. Full-service. 1.5% back at closing. Call us to start the conversation — no obligation.
